texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Vitals/Measurements Release Notes
texts.text: Vitals/Measurements Release Notes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This release has been checked for 508 compliance as a component of CPRS. The entire application is scheduled for a compliance review in the near future.  Any compliance issues found during the review will be corrected by a patch after the review.
texts.text: This release has been checked for 508 compliance as a component of CPRS. The entire application is scheduled for a compliance review in the near future.  Any compliance issues found during the review will be corrected by a patch after the review.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch provides the following functionality changes:
texts.text: This patch provides the following functionality changes:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: This patch provides a Dynamic Link Library (DLL) file that will be called from CPRS v26. This DLL provides a Graphical User Interface (GUI) called Vitals Lite; Vitals Lite replaces the CPRS functionality for entering and displaying patient vitals data, and adds new vitals-related functionality for the CPRS user.
texts.text: This patch provides a Dynamic Link Library (DLL) file that will be called from CPRS v26. This DLL provides a Graphical User Interface (GUI) called Vitals Lite; Vitals Lite replaces the CPRS functionality for entering and displaying patient vitals data, and adds new vitals-related functionality for the CPRS user.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When the user clicks on the vitals portion of the CPRS Coversheet, the Vitals Lite window opens. The user will be able to:
texts.text: When the user clicks on the vitals portion of the CPRS Coversheet, the Vitals Lite window opens. The user will be able to:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: View patient vitals data in a graph and grid format.
texts.text: View patient vitals data in a graph and grid format.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Select date ranges for retrieving data
texts.text: Select date ranges for retrieving data

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Select vital types to plot on the graph
texts.text: Select vital types to plot on the graph

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Select Intake and Output (I&O) values to plot on the graph (liquid values only)
texts.text: Select Intake and Output (I&O) values to plot on the graph (liquid values only)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Use zoom features to view the graph data
texts.text: Use zoom features to view the graph data

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Display the numerical values on the graph
texts.text: Display the numerical values on the graph

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Display the graph in 3-d format
texts.text: Display the graph in 3-d format

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Print a graph to a Windows-type printer
texts.text: Print a graph to a Windows-type printer

texts.label: text
texts.orig: View the qualifier abbreviations along with the numerical values in the grid (new)
texts.text: View the qualifier abbreviations along with the numerical values in the grid (new)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: View the name of the person who entered the data and the hospital location associated with the record in the grid, except for I&O values (new)
texts.text: View the name of the person who entered the data and the hospital location associated with the record in the grid, except for I&O values (new)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Change the background color of the graph (new)
texts.text: Change the background color of the graph (new)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Enter vitals data using locally-configured input templates (new)
texts.text: Enter vitals data using locally-configured input templates (new)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Mark existing records as entered-in-error (new)
texts.text: Mark existing records as entered-in-error (new)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Display patient allergy data (new)
texts.text: Display patient allergy data (new)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For CPRS users who have access to the Encounter button, the Enter Vitals window can also be opened from the Notes tab. On the Notes tab, the user clicks on the "Encounter" button to select or create an encounter, then the "Vitals" tab on the encounter form, and the "Enter Vitals" button to invoke the DLL. The user will be able to enter vitals data using existing input templates.
texts.text: For CPRS users who have access to the Encounter button, the Enter Vitals window can also be opened from the Notes tab. On the Notes tab, the user clicks on the "Encounter" button to select or create an encounter, then the "Vitals" tab on the encounter form, and the "Enter Vitals" button to invoke the DLL. The user will be able to enter vitals data using existing input templates.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If CPRS v26 is not installed, this DLL will merely reside on the system. It will not adversely affect CPRS v25. This DLL is not called by the Vitals.exe or VitalsManager.exe files that make up the Vitals Graphical User Interface (GUI).
texts.text: If CPRS v26 is not installed, this DLL will merely reside on the system. It will not adversely affect CPRS v25. This DLL is not called by the Vitals.exe or VitalsManager.exe files that make up the Vitals Graphical User Interface (GUI).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: This patch makes the existing Vitals GUI (Vitals.exe) compliant with the Clinical Context Object Workgroup (CCOW) standard. When the Vitals GUI is invoked, by default, it is CCOW-enabled.
texts.text: This patch makes the existing Vitals GUI (Vitals.exe) compliant with the Clinical Context Object Workgroup (CCOW) standard. When the Vitals GUI is invoked, by default, it is CCOW-enabled.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It checks to see what other GUIs are currently running on the user’s workstation. It sends current user and patient information to the CCOW “vault” which coordinates user and patient information for the GUIs that are running. If another CCOW-enabled application is running when the Vitals GUI is invoked, the Vitals GUI will open automatically without the need for a second sign-on. If a patient is already selected in the open GUI, the Vitals GUI will open with the patient being used by the open application.
texts.text: It checks to see what other GUIs are currently running on the user’s workstation. It sends current user and patient information to the CCOW “vault” which coordinates user and patient information for the GUIs that are running. If another CCOW-enabled application is running when the Vitals GUI is invoked, the Vitals GUI will open automatically without the need for a second sign-on. If a patient is already selected in the open GUI, the Vitals GUI will open with the patient being used by the open application.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NOTE:
texts.text: NOTE:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CCOW may not work properly if you use VPN to access the VA network remotely. This is a known issue: if you receive an error message when you  use CCOW-compliant applications via VPN, the current workaround is to disable the context. If you are using multiple  applications while CCOW is disabled, please be sure to verify the active patient when switching applications.
texts.text: CCOW may not work properly if you use VPN to access the VA network remotely. This is a known issue: if you receive an error message when you  use CCOW-compliant applications via VPN, the current workaround is to disable the context. If you are using multiple  applications while CCOW is disabled, please be sure to verify the active patient when switching applications.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:
Users can completely disable the CCOW functionality at start up time by adding a parameter setting to the shortcut used to invoke the Vitals GUI. This will force the user to sign on and select a patient when invoking the Vitals GUI.

To disable CCOW functionality:
texts.text:
Users can completely disable the CCOW functionality at start up time by adding a parameter setting to the shortcut used to invoke the Vitals GUI. This will force the user to sign on and select a patient when invoking the Vitals GUI.

To disable CCOW functionality:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Right click on the Vitals icon on your desktop
texts.text: Right click on the Vitals icon on your desktop

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select the Properties option
texts.text: Select the Properties option

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select the Shortcut tab
texts.text: Select the Shortcut tab

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Go to the Target textbox
texts.text: Go to the Target textbox

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Add a space to the end of the text followed by “/NOCCOW” without the quotes
texts.text: Add a space to the end of the text followed by “/NOCCOW” without the quotes

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Click Apply, and then click OK.
texts.text: Click Apply, and then click OK.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Example of Target textbox:
texts.text: Example of Target textbox:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: “C:\Program Files\vista\Vitals\Vitals.exe" /NOCCOW
texts.text: “C:\Program Files\vista\Vitals\Vitals.exe" /NOCCOW

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Users can disable the automatic sign on, but allow the automatic selection of a patient by adding a different parameter setting to the shortcut used to invoke the Vitals GUI. This will force the user to sign on. If a patient is already selected in an open GUI, the Vitals GUI will open automatically with the patient being used by that open application.
texts.text: Users can disable the automatic sign on, but allow the automatic selection of a patient by adding a different parameter setting to the shortcut used to invoke the Vitals GUI. This will force the user to sign on. If a patient is already selected in an open GUI, the Vitals GUI will open automatically with the patient being used by that open application.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To disable the automatic sign on, but keep the automatic patient selection:
texts.text: To disable the automatic sign on, but keep the automatic patient selection:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Right click on the Vitals icon on your desktop
texts.text: Right click on the Vitals icon on your desktop

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select the Properties option
texts.text: Select the Properties option

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select the Shortcut tab
texts.text: Select the Shortcut tab

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Go to the Target textbox
texts.text: Go to the Target textbox

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Add a space to the end of the text followed by “CCOW=PATIENTONLY” without the quotes
texts.text: Add a space to the end of the text followed by “CCOW=PATIENTONLY” without the quotes

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Click Apply, and then click OK.
texts.text: Click Apply, and then click OK.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Example of Target textbox:
texts.text: Example of Target textbox:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: “C:\Program Files\vista\Vitals\Vitals.exe" /CCOW=PATIENTONLY
texts.text: “C:\Program Files\vista\Vitals\Vitals.exe" /CCOW=PATIENTONLY

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: This patch provides a new Application Programming Interface (API) for use by the Health Summary (HS) package. This new API returns non-numeric values (e.g., Refused) for display in the HS ad hoc reports. HS patch GMTS*2.7*78 must also be installed for the non-numeric values to appear in the HS ad hoc reports.
texts.text: This patch provides a new Application Programming Interface (API) for use by the Health Summary (HS) package. This new API returns non-numeric values (e.g., Refused) for display in the HS ad hoc reports. HS patch GMTS*2.7*78 must also be installed for the non-numeric values to appear in the HS ad hoc reports.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The following changes have been made to the Vitals GUI main screen:
texts.text: The following changes have been made to the Vitals GUI main screen:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Updates have been made to increase compatibility with assistive technology. Keyboard shortcuts and navigation options are added to make the GUI accessible to a wider range of users, including those who have limited dexterity, low vision, or other disabilities.
texts.text: Updates have been made to increase compatibility with assistive technology. Keyboard shortcuts and navigation options are added to make the GUI accessible to a wider range of users, including those who have limited dexterity, low vision, or other disabilities.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Patient identification information is now displayed in the title bar.
texts.text: Patient identification information is now displayed in the title bar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The patient identification button in the navigation bar is now accessible by using the <Tab> key. Pressing the <Enter> or <Return> key while this button is active will open the Patient Inquiry report window.
texts.text: The patient identification button in the navigation bar is now accessible by using the <Tab> key. Pressing the <Enter> or <Return> key while this button is active will open the Patient Inquiry report window.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: New options are available in the FILE menu:
texts.text: New options are available in the FILE menu:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patient Inquiry – This option allows the user to view patient demographic information in a separate report window.
texts.text: Patient Inquiry – This option allows the user to view patient demographic information in a separate report window.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Data Grid Report – This option allows the user to view the information from the data grid in a text-based report format.
texts.text: Data Grid Report – This option allows the user to view the information from the data grid in a text-based report format.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Rejoin Clinical Link – This option allows the user to join the CCOW context, using one of the following two options. If you want the other open applications to synchronize with the current patient in the active application, select “Use this Application’s Data.” If you want the current application to synchronize with the patient that is open in another application, select “Use Global Data.”
texts.text: Rejoin Clinical Link – This option allows the user to join the CCOW context, using one of the following two options. If you want the other open applications to synchronize with the current patient in the active application, select “Use this Application’s Data.” If you want the current application to synchronize with the patient that is open in another application, select “Use Global Data.”

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Break the Clinical Link – This option allows the user to break the CCOW context and use any patient regardless of the patient selected in the CCOW application.
texts.text: Break the Clinical Link – This option allows the user to break the CCOW context and use any patient regardless of the patient selected in the CCOW application.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Show Status – This option allows the user to view information on CCOW, such as whether or not the Contextor software has been installed, and whether the application is participating in a clinical context.
texts.text: Show Status – This option allows the user to view information on CCOW, such as whether or not the Contextor software has been installed, and whether the application is participating in a clinical context.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Show/Hide Graph Options – This option toggles between showing and hiding graph functions.
texts.text: Show/Hide Graph Options – This option toggles between showing and hiding graph functions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Select Graph Color – This option allows the user to change the background color of the data graph.
texts.text: Select Graph Color – This option allows the user to change the background color of the data graph.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Print Graph – This option allows the user to print the graph to a Windows-type printer.
texts.text: Print Graph – This option allows the user to print the graph to a Windows-type printer.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The patient selection buttons for "Ward", "Unit", "Team", "Clinic" and "All" are changed from radio buttons to ordinary buttons.
texts.text: The patient selection buttons for "Ward", "Unit", "Team", "Clinic" and "All" are changed from radio buttons to ordinary buttons.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: An "Input Vitals for the Selected Patients" button is added to the bottom of the patient selector pane in the main Vitals window. When the user selects more than one patient at a time, this button must be clicked to enter vitals data for those patients. If only one patient is selected, only the "Enter Vitals" button will be available.
texts.text: An "Input Vitals for the Selected Patients" button is added to the bottom of the patient selector pane in the main Vitals window. When the user selects more than one patient at a time, this button must be clicked to enter vitals data for those patients. If only one patient is selected, only the "Enter Vitals" button will be available.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: New icons are added to the graph display to allow zooming functionality. A text box allows the user to select the percentage of change when zooming. The default is 10.
texts.text: New icons are added to the graph display to allow zooming functionality. A text box allows the user to select the percentage of change when zooming. The default is 10.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The data grid shows the name of the person who entered the data and the hospital location associated with the record, except for I&O values.
texts.text: The data grid shows the name of the person who entered the data and the hospital location associated with the record, except for I&O values.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 2.Description of Waiver Request:
texts.text: 2.Description of Waiver Request:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch has been tested in production accounts since late August 2005. The latest test version, T19, was sent to the test sites on March 21, 2006. The goal was to make T19 the final version. Since then, several minor problems have been discovered. To fix these problems requires a new test version along with an extra week of testing. This patch is required by CPRS v26. This patch should be installed about one week before CPRS v26 so that sites can have adequate time to place the Dynamic Link Library (DLL) file that comes with this patch to the necessary servers and workstations where CPRS v26 will look for it. CPRS v26 is slated for release on May 5, 2006. Therefore, we wish to release T19 as is and we will fix these defects in a future patch.
texts.text: This patch has been tested in production accounts since late August 2005. The latest test version, T19, was sent to the test sites on March 21, 2006. The goal was to make T19 the final version. Since then, several minor problems have been discovered. To fix these problems requires a new test version along with an extra week of testing. This patch is required by CPRS v26. This patch should be installed about one week before CPRS v26 so that sites can have adequate time to place the Dynamic Link Library (DLL) file that comes with this patch to the necessary servers and workstations where CPRS v26 will look for it. CPRS v26 is slated for release on May 5, 2006. Therefore, we wish to release T19 as is and we will fix these defects in a future patch.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Problem Reporting System Number: CQ 11263
texts.text: Problem Reporting System Number: CQ 11263

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Problem Reporting System Description: The “Data Grid Report” displays only the values that are visible in the data grid on the screen. If the selected date range includes twenty columns of data, but only six columns appear on the screen, then only the data from the visible six columns appear in the “Data Grid Report” screen.
texts.text: Problem Reporting System Description: The “Data Grid Report” displays only the values that are visible in the data grid on the screen. If the selected date range includes twenty columns of data, but only six columns appear on the screen, then only the data from the visible six columns appear in the “Data Grid Report” screen.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Impact: There is little or no impact to users who are not visually impaired which is the major number of users of the software. There is an impact to visually impaired users who may not realize that only a subset of records from the data grid are displayed in the “Data Grid Report” screen.
texts.text: Impact: There is little or no impact to users who are not visually impaired which is the major number of users of the software. There is an impact to visually impaired users who may not realize that only a subset of records from the data grid are displayed in the “Data Grid Report” screen.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Workaround: The user can change the focus of the data grid to highlight other records and rerun the “Data Grid Report” to display other subsets of the data.
texts.text: Workaround: The user can change the focus of the data grid to highlight other records and rerun the “Data Grid Report” to display other subsets of the data.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Comments: The workaround is possible, but not intuitive to a visually impaired user. Some additional thought and input is needed especially from visually impaired users to improve this feature. Rather than create a hasty solution or delay the release of the patch, we would like to gather further input and solutions and fix the problem in the next GUI-related patch.
texts.text: Comments: The workaround is possible, but not intuitive to a visually impaired user. Some additional thought and input is needed especially from visually impaired users to improve this feature. Rather than create a hasty solution or delay the release of the patch, we would like to gather further input and solutions and fix the problem in the next GUI-related patch.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Problem Reporting System Description: When there are too many qualifiers in a data grid cell, you cannot always see the rightmost text in the cell and the cell cannot be expanded.
texts.text: Problem Reporting System Description: When there are too many qualifiers in a data grid cell, you cannot always see the rightmost text in the cell and the cell cannot be expanded.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Impact: Vital types that allow three or four qualifiers are mostly affected by this problem.
texts.text: Impact: Vital types that allow three or four qualifiers are mostly affected by this problem.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Workaround: Users can click on the vital type name above the data graph to open another screen which displays all of the qualifiers information for the records in the data grid.
texts.text: Workaround: Users can click on the vital type name above the data graph to open another screen which displays all of the qualifiers information for the records in the data grid.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Comments: We will research possible solutions such as making the data cells wider or scrollable.
texts.text: Comments: We will research possible solutions such as making the data cells wider or scrollable.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Problem Reporting System Description: When entering a Pulse Oximetry value in Vitals User and Vitals Lite, the user can enter a flow rate and concentration percentage value with a qualifier of “Room Air”. The “Room Air” qualifier implies that the patient was not receiving oxygen so there should not be a flow rate or concentration percentage value entered. There are software checks to prevent this from happening, but there is one way around those checks. Select “Room Air” first, click the OK button, go back into the drop down list and enter a flow rate or concentration percent value, and do not touch the textbox with the “Room Air” qualifier.
texts.text: Problem Reporting System Description: When entering a Pulse Oximetry value in Vitals User and Vitals Lite, the user can enter a flow rate and concentration percentage value with a qualifier of “Room Air”. The “Room Air” qualifier implies that the patient was not receiving oxygen so there should not be a flow rate or concentration percentage value entered. There are software checks to prevent this from happening, but there is one way around those checks. Select “Room Air” first, click the OK button, go back into the drop down list and enter a flow rate or concentration percent value, and do not touch the textbox with the “Room Air” qualifier.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Impact: The impact is a user may wonder why there is a flow rate or oxygen concentration when the patient is on “Room Air”.
texts.text: Impact: The impact is a user may wonder why there is a flow rate or oxygen concentration when the patient is on “Room Air”.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Workaround: If the user re-selects “Room Air” before saving the record, the flow rate and oxygen concentration values are removed.
texts.text: Workaround: If the user re-selects “Room Air” before saving the record, the flow rate and oxygen concentration values are removed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Comments: We will fix this in the next patch.
texts.text: Comments: We will fix this in the next patch.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Problem Reporting System Description:  When editing qualifiers in Vitals User’s “Edit User Templates” option, an access violation and list out of bounds error can occur. To create the problem select a vital type that has qualifiers, make some changes, save the changes, change the qualifiers without re-selecting the vital type and save the change. This generates an access violation and/or list out of bounds error.
texts.text: Problem Reporting System Description:  When editing qualifiers in Vitals User’s “Edit User Templates” option, an access violation and list out of bounds error can occur. To create the problem select a vital type that has qualifiers, make some changes, save the changes, change the qualifiers without re-selecting the vital type and save the change. This generates an access violation and/or list out of bounds error.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Impact: The user has to close the template editor screen and re-open to do any further editing. The impact should be small. Input templates are not created or edited very often. In some facilities, the creation and editing of templates is limited to Clinical Applications Coordinators (CACs) only.
texts.text: Impact: The user has to close the template editor screen and re-open to do any further editing. The impact should be small. Input templates are not created or edited very often. In some facilities, the creation and editing of templates is limited to Clinical Applications Coordinators (CACs) only.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Workaround: The workaround is to close the screen and re-enter it.
texts.text: Workaround: The workaround is to close the screen and re-enter it.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Comments: This can be fixed in the next patch.
texts.text: Comments: This can be fixed in the next patch.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Problem Reporting System Description: When the Vitals User interface is run, “All Results” are selected for the date range and the “Data Grid Report” is run, an extra line may appear in the report display. That extra line contains a date and time, but no vitals signs data after it. The date and time value is erroneous. There is no data for the patient with this date and time.
texts.text: Problem Reporting System Description: When the Vitals User interface is run, “All Results” are selected for the date range and the “Data Grid Report” is run, an extra line may appear in the report display. That extra line contains a date and time, but no vitals signs data after it. The date and time value is erroneous. There is no data for the patient with this date and time.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Impact: This may be confusing to the user who will wonder why there are no values following the date/time.
texts.text: Impact: This may be confusing to the user who will wonder why there are no values following the date/time.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Workaround: This happens when the date range is “All Results”. The date range default is 7 days for an inpatient and 6 months for an outpatient. Users can use the defaults. A user may want to see all data for a patient, but will mostly want to see only recent values.
texts.text: Workaround: This happens when the date range is “All Results”. The date range default is 7 days for an inpatient and 6 months for an outpatient. Users can use the defaults. A user may want to see all data for a patient, but will mostly want to see only recent values.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Comments: We will fix this in the next patch when we fix issue 4.1.
texts.text: Comments: We will fix this in the next patch when we fix issue 4.1.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Problem Reporting System Description: The “Print Qualifiers Table” report in the Vitals Manager module will error out if the user selects a printer that does not allow queuing. The option should not allow the user to select printers that do not allow queuing of the output.
texts.text: Problem Reporting System Description: The “Print Qualifiers Table” report in the Vitals Manager module will error out if the user selects a printer that does not allow queuing. The option should not allow the user to select printers that do not allow queuing of the output.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Impact: The impact is very low. The Vitals Manager module is limited to a very small number of users such as Clinical Application Coordinators (CACs) and Information Resource Management Services (IRMS) users. This report is not a regular or commonly printed report.
texts.text: Impact: The impact is very low. The Vitals Manager module is limited to a very small number of users such as Clinical Application Coordinators (CACs) and Information Resource Management Services (IRMS) users. This report is not a regular or commonly printed report.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Workaround: The workaround is to select a printer that does allow queuing of output.
texts.text: Workaround: The workaround is to select a printer that does allow queuing of output.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Comments: We will fix this in the next patch.
texts.text: Comments: We will fix this in the next patch.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Problem Reporting System Description: The slider bar above the data grid can be used to traverse grid. Moving the pointer on the slider bar to the right will display more recent records in the grid. Moving the pointer to the left, will display older records. At times, the pointer on the slider bar is on the far left end of the slider even though there are older records in the grid. The user must drag the pointer to the right to see those older records. This is counter to how the slider bar should work.
texts.text: Problem Reporting System Description: The slider bar above the data grid can be used to traverse grid. Moving the pointer on the slider bar to the right will display more recent records in the grid. Moving the pointer to the left, will display older records. At times, the pointer on the slider bar is on the far left end of the slider even though there are older records in the grid. The user must drag the pointer to the right to see those older records. This is counter to how the slider bar should work.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Impact: The impact is low. The problem does not always occur.
texts.text: Impact: The impact is low. The problem does not always occur.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Workaround: The user can still drag the pointer to traverse the data in the grid.
texts.text: Workaround: The user can still drag the pointer to traverse the data in the grid.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Comments: We will fix this in the next patch.
texts.text: Comments: We will fix this in the next patch.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Justification of Request:
texts.text: Justification of Request:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: These issues were discovered after the final version of the patch was made. We feel the defects are minor and have little impact on the users or the quality of the data. Therefore, these defects should not delay the release of the patch. This patch is required for CPRS v26 which is also near to release. Any delay in the GMRV*5.0*3 patch may impact the release of CPRS v26.
texts.text: These issues were discovered after the final version of the patch was made. We feel the defects are minor and have little impact on the users or the quality of the data. Therefore, these defects should not delay the release of the patch. This patch is required for CPRS v26 which is also near to release. Any delay in the GMRV*5.0*3 patch may impact the release of CPRS v26.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 6.	Plan for Addressing Known Defects:
texts.text: 6.	Plan for Addressing Known Defects:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We feel these defects can be addressed in the next patch that modifies the Vitals Graphical User Interfaces.
texts.text: We feel these defects can be addressed in the next patch that modifies the Vitals Graphical User Interfaces.
